objective: Build and stabilize a multi-tenant SaaS system (AI Growth OS) with authentication, billing, usage tracking, quota enforcement, and frontend dashboard with login and analytics capabilities
decisions: Continue system strictly from existing phase state without redesign
Use existing project structure instead of creating duplicate modules (jwt_guard instead of jwt_handler)
Maintain separation of routes (tenant_admin_routes vs system_admin_routes)
Implement JWT-based authentication with role and tenant_id embedded
Use database-driven tenant and API key model for SaaS control
Enforce quota using api_keys table (used vs quota)
Replace print statements with logging for production readiness
Use React frontend with token stored in localStorage
Handle null token cases in frontend to prevent runtime crashes
Adopt VS Code instead of terminal editor for code stability
Do not redesign architecture; extend existing modules only
built_components: PostgreSQL database with tables: users, tenants, api_keys, invoices, audit_logs, usage_logs
Authentication system (signup + login with hashed password)
JWT token generation including user_id, tenant_id, role
Role-based access control with admin guard
Tenant management system (create tenant, API key generation)
Billing system (invoice creation, order creation, payment confirmation)
Quota enforcement logic in billing route
System admin routes for usage aggregation
React frontend dashboard (health, billing, invoice table)
Frontend login UI with token handling
Docker-based deployment setup with API, DB, Redis, worker, monitoring stack
inputs: User commands via terminal (curl, docker, psql)
User-provided code snippets (FastAPI routes, React components)
API requests to billing and admin endpoints
outputs: JWT access token from login endpoint
Error messages (404, CORS, import errors, React runtime errors)
notes: Backend SaaS system (authentication, billing, usage tracking, quota enforcement) is fully operational and validated through Module 9. Frontend is functional but incomplete: login flow requires user creation via signup, analytics UI (usage and audit logs) not yet implemented, and admin control UI is minimal. Multiple issues resolved during build including docker misconfiguration, import path errors, React hook misuse, null token handling, and CORS restrictions.
